name: Watchlist
description: Bookmark any movie or series from its detail page. Bookmarked items appear in a home-screen row and a dedicated Watchlist tab. Items auto-remove when marked as played.
overview: Per-user watchlist with home-screen row and library tab.

versions.changelog: Watchlist tab cards: footer title/year now link to detail page. Cards set to 170px width (matches Jellyfin portrait card size). Image fetched at maxHeight=450 for sharper display.

Watchlist tab: manual portrait card rendering with movie poster, title, year, and per-card Remove button. Removed cardBuilder dependency (not available in Jellyfin 10.11 webpack build). Cards match native Jellyfin library layout.
Watchlist tab now matches Jellyfin Movies library layout: padded-top/left/right page container, sectionTitleContainer-cards header, itemsContainer vertical-wrap grid with cardBuilder portrait cards.

Diagnostic: log loadWatchlistTab entry (element found/null, wlLoaded, children count) on every tick so we can see why #watchlistTabPage is not being populated. Also retry if wlLoaded but children=0 (prior run failed before building DOM).
Tab trigger changed to #watchlistTabPage. watchlist.js now builds all child HTML (h2, loading, empty, grid) itself. CustomTabs HTML setting needs only:

Move watchlist tab rendering into watchlist.js. CustomTabs stores HTML as static content — inline scripts don't execute. watchlist.js now watches for #wlGrid in the DOM and populates it via loadWatchlistTab(). Paste skeleton HTML into CustomTabs settings (see comment in watchlist.js). Falls back to plain text list if cardBuilder unavailable.

Fix watchlist tab: use full X-Emby-Authorization header (old format Authorization: MediaBrowser Token=... was failing). Handle getCurrentUserId vs currentUserId across web versions. Use apiVal() helper for getter/method duality. Better error display in tab.

Diagnostic: bypass [Authorize] middleware, use IAuthorizationContext directly for all endpoints. Logs the raw X-Emby-Authorization header and IAuthorizationContext result to Jellyfin logs on every request. JS now logs full auth header and uses apiVal() helper to handle deviceId/appName as either functions or getter properties.

Fix X-Emby-Authorization header format. v1.0.15.0 joined "MediaBrowser" into the array with .join(", ") producing "MediaBrowser, Client=..." but Jellyfin requires "MediaBrowser Client=..." (space not comma). CustomAuthenticationHandler rejected the malformed header with 401.

Replace ApiClient.ajax with native fetch + explicit X-Emby-Authorization header. ApiClient.ajax's _serverInfo.AccessToken was null in this 10.11 environment, silently producing 401. Now extract token directly (accessToken() -> _serverInfo -> currentUser) and build full header. Switch controller to [Authorize(AuthenticationSchemes="CustomAuthentication")] to match what Jellyfin's auth scheme is actually registered as.

Use [Authorize(Policy = "FirstTimeSetupOrDefault")]. v1.0.12.0's "DefaultAuthorization" doesn't exist in 10.11; caused 500 InvalidOperationException. FirstTimeSetupOrDefault is the policy Jellyfin's own normal-user controllers (LibraryController, LocalizationController) use. Drop [ResponseCache] from inject.js endpoint.

Switch controller [Authorize] -> [Authorize(Policy = "DefaultAuthorization")]. Bare [Authorize] picks Jellyfin's fallback policy that rejects normal users with 401; DefaultAuthorization admits any authenticated user.

Use ApiClient.ajax for Watchlist API calls. Raw fetch with Authorization: MediaBrowser Token=... was rejected as 401 on Jellyfin 10.11; ApiClient.ajax constructs the full X-Emby-Authorization header (Client, Device, DeviceId, Version, Token) the server expects.

Match Jellyfin 10.11 detail-page favorite button: button.btnUserRating / is="emby-ratingbutton". Mirror the detailButton-content wrapper structure so the bookmark button matches the native layout exactly.

Bookmark icons now render. Add Jellyfin 10.11 favorite-button selector (button.btnUserData[data-method=markFavorite]). Inherit className and icon span from the native favorite button so the bookmark sizes/aligns natively. Cache-bust script src and meta marker to v1.0.9.0.

Fix bookmark icon injection. FileTransformation keys callback pipelines by exact pattern string with direct dict-lookup priority. Other IAmParadox27 plugins register under literal "index.html"; we were using "index\.html" -> separate unreachable pipeline -> Transform never invoked. Switch to literal pattern so callback joins the running pipeline.

Diagnostic: replace Console.WriteLine with cached ILogger in IndexHtmlTransformer so Transform invocations show up in Jellyfin logs. Inject extra <meta name="watchlist-plugin-marker" content="1.0.7.0"> tag for independent DOM verification.

Diagnostic: log every IndexHtmlTransformer.Transform invocation to Jellyfin stdout. Cache-bust script src with ?v=1.0.6.0. Stronger no-cache headers (no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, max-age=0, Pragma, Expires).

Integrate with IAmParadox27 File Transformation plugin. Other IAmParadox27 plugins inject scripts via File Transformation; raw middleware loses the race against it. Now we register a transformation callback the same way they do, via reflection. Middleware kept as fallback.

Diagnostic build with heavy console logging. Multi-strategy itemId extraction, multi-selector favorite button detection (.btnUserDataFavorite, .btnFavorite, [data-action=favorite], etc.), React Router pushState/replaceState patching. Open browser console to trace every step under [Watchlist] tag.

Rewrite injection to mirror BORNIOS/JellyTrend: drop IHostedService file patcher, serve watchlist.js as embedded resource at /Watchlist/watchlist.js, inject before </head>, skip buffered fallback if Content-Encoding is set. csproj uses ExcludeAssets=runtime + FrameworkReference Microsoft.AspNetCore.App.
Add IndexHtmlPatcher: IHostedService that directly patches index.html on disk at startup, bypassing ASP.NET pipeline ordering issues. Expand middleware path matching. Add debug logging.
Fix bookmark icon injection: switch BookmarkInjectionMiddleware to IMiddleware pattern (DI-resolved per request) and add direct-from-disk primary path. Script now served via /Watchlist/inject.js.
Initial release.
